docs.text: Welcome to the Florida Health Charts Web Scraper This package scrapes data from the Florida Health Charts Opioid use dashboard located here . It scrapes the data for all counties and all years currently up to 2020. Project Layout data/ # data folder for finalized collected data docs/ # documentation src/fl_health_scraper # main package tests/ # tests
docs.title: Home

docs.text: This package scrapes data from the Florida Health Charts Opioid use dashboard located here . It scrapes the data for all counties and all years currently up to 2020.
docs.title: Welcome to the Florida Health Charts Web Scraper

docs.text: Sample Mkdocstrings Modules fl_health_scraper.constants This module contains globals for the entire project. COUNTIES A dict of county names and their form values. URL Base url for requests. YEARS Years to query for. fl_health_scraper.core This module is the core functionality of the scraper. combine_files () Combines all of the individual files into one composite file. Source code in fl_health_scraper/core.py def combine_files () -> pd . DataFrame : """Combines all of the individual files into one composite file.""" large_df = pd . concat (( pd . read_csv ( f ) for f in generate_file_names ())) new_col_order = [ "Year" , "County" , "Indicator" , "Measure" , "Jan-Mar" , "Apr-June" , "July-Sep" , "Oct-Dec" , "Year-to-Date" , "Case Definition" , ] reordered_df = large_df [ new_col_order ] reordered_df . to_csv ( os . path . join ( "data" , "composite.csv" ), index = False ) return reordered_df export_data ( data , county_name , year ) Exports data to a file. Parameters: Name Type Description Default data dict data scraped from website required year int year of dataset required county_name str county name of dataset required Source code in fl_health_scraper/core.py def export_data ( data : dict [ str , list [ str ]], county_name : str , year : int , ) -> pd . DataFrame : """Exports data to a file. Args: data: data scraped from website year: year of dataset county_name: county name of dataset """ df = pd . DataFrame . from_dict ( data ) df [ "Year" ] = year df [ "County" ] = county_name directory = os . path . join ( "data" , str ( year )) if not os . path . exists ( directory ): os . mkdir ( directory ) df . to_csv ( os . path . join ( directory , f " { county_name } .csv" ), index = False ) return df gather_data () Runs entire pipeline of visiting site, scraping data, and exporting data. Source code in fl_health_scraper/core.py def gather_data () -> pd . DataFrame : """Runs entire pipeline of visiting site, scraping data, and exporting data.""" for i , year in enumerate ( YEARS ): print ( f "Getting data for { year } -- { i + 1 } / { len ( YEARS ) } " ) for name , id in COUNTIES . items (): site_content = visit_site ( URL , id , year ) site_data = scrape_site ( site_content ) export_data ( data = site_data , year = year , county_name = name ) df = combine_files () remove_files () return df generate_file_names () Generates filenames for export using YEARS and COUNTIES globals. Returns: Type Description list list[str]: list of filepaths/filenames. Source code in fl_health_scraper/core.py def generate_file_names () -> list [ str ]: """Generates filenames for export using YEARS and COUNTIES globals. Returns: list[str]: list of filepaths/filenames. """ file_names = [] for year in YEARS : for name , _ in COUNTIES . items (): file_names . append ( os . path . join ( "data" , str ( year ), f " { name } .csv" )) return file_names remove_files () Removes files and dirs created during scraping process. Source code in fl_health_scraper/core.py def remove_files () -> bool : """Removes files and dirs created during scraping process.""" year_dirs = [ os . path . join ( "data" , str ( y )) for y in YEARS ] years_dirs_exist = all ( os . path . exists ( d ) for d in year_dirs ) if not os . path . exists ( "data" ) or not years_dirs_exist : return False else : filenames = generate_file_names () for f in filenames : if os . path . exists ( f ): os . remove ( f ) if years_dirs_exist : for d in year_dirs : os . rmdir ( d ) return True scrape_site ( web_response ) Scrapes webpage content from web response context. Parameters: Name Type Description Default content website content from response object required Returns: Type Description dict dict[str, list[str]]: dictionary of column names and data scraped from the web-table Source code in fl_health_scraper/core.py def scrape_site ( web_response : requests . Response ) -> dict [ str , list [ str ]]: """Scrapes webpage content from web response context. Args: content: website content from response object Returns: dict[str, list[str]]: dictionary of column names and data scraped from the web-table """ content = web_response . content soup = BeautifulSoup ( content , "html.parser" ) table = soup . find ( id = "dtOpioidProfile" ) data : dict [ str , list [ str ]] = { "Indicator" : [], "Measure" : [], "Year" : [], "Jan-Mar" : [], "Apr-June" : [], "July-Sep" : [], "Oct-Dec" : [], "Year-to-Date" : [], "Case Definition" : [], } # get table data directly using regex compiled 'id' attribute regex_columns : list [ str ] = [ "colIndTitle_Row" , "colMeasure_Row" , "colYear_Row" , "colQuarter1_Row" , "colQuarter2_Row" , "colQuarter3_Row" , "colQuarter4_Row" , "colAnnual_Row" , "colCaseDefinition_Row" , ] # these loops can be improved (readability, refactored into funcs etc.) for field , col in zip ( data . keys (), regex_columns ): data . update ( { field : [ r . text . strip () for r in table . find_all ( id = re . compile ( f " { col } " ))]} ) return data visit_site ( url , county_id , year ) Visit a specified site using post-request and return response content. Parameters: Name Type Description Default url str base url to visit required county_id int numeric id of county to query from form data required year int year to query in form data required Returns: Type Description Response bytes: response content Source code in fl_health_scraper/core.py def visit_site ( url : str , county_id : int , year : int ) -> requests . Response : """Visit a specified site using post-request and return response content. Args: url: base url to visit county_id: numeric id of county to query from form data year: year to query in form data Returns: bytes: response content """ response = requests . post ( url , data = { "islCounty" : county_id , "islYears" : year }) return response

docs.text: Combines all of the individual files into one composite file. Source code in fl_health_scraper/core.py def combine_files () -> pd . DataFrame : """Combines all of the individual files into one composite file.""" large_df = pd . concat (( pd . read_csv ( f ) for f in generate_file_names ())) new_col_order = [ "Year" , "County" , "Indicator" , "Measure" , "Jan-Mar" , "Apr-June" , "July-Sep" , "Oct-Dec" , "Year-to-Date" , "Case Definition" , ] reordered_df = large_df [ new_col_order ] reordered_df . to_csv ( os . path . join ( "data" , "composite.csv" ), index = False ) return reordered_df
docs.title: combine_files()

docs.text: Exports data to a file. Parameters: Name Type Description Default data dict data scraped from website required year int year of dataset required county_name str county name of dataset required Source code in fl_health_scraper/core.py def export_data ( data : dict [ str , list [ str ]], county_name : str , year : int , ) -> pd . DataFrame : """Exports data to a file. Args: data: data scraped from website year: year of dataset county_name: county name of dataset """ df = pd . DataFrame . from_dict ( data ) df [ "Year" ] = year df [ "County" ] = county_name directory = os . path . join ( "data" , str ( year )) if not os . path . exists ( directory ): os . mkdir ( directory ) df . to_csv ( os . path . join ( directory , f " { county_name } .csv" ), index = False ) return df
docs.title: export_data()

docs.text: Runs entire pipeline of visiting site, scraping data, and exporting data. Source code in fl_health_scraper/core.py def gather_data () -> pd . DataFrame : """Runs entire pipeline of visiting site, scraping data, and exporting data.""" for i , year in enumerate ( YEARS ): print ( f "Getting data for { year } -- { i + 1 } / { len ( YEARS ) } " ) for name , id in COUNTIES . items (): site_content = visit_site ( URL , id , year ) site_data = scrape_site ( site_content ) export_data ( data = site_data , year = year , county_name = name ) df = combine_files () remove_files () return df
docs.title: gather_data()

docs.text: Generates filenames for export using YEARS and COUNTIES globals. Returns: Type Description list list[str]: list of filepaths/filenames. Source code in fl_health_scraper/core.py def generate_file_names () -> list [ str ]: """Generates filenames for export using YEARS and COUNTIES globals. Returns: list[str]: list of filepaths/filenames. """ file_names = [] for year in YEARS : for name , _ in COUNTIES . items (): file_names . append ( os . path . join ( "data" , str ( year ), f " { name } .csv" )) return file_names
docs.title: generate_file_names()

docs.text: Removes files and dirs created during scraping process. Source code in fl_health_scraper/core.py def remove_files () -> bool : """Removes files and dirs created during scraping process.""" year_dirs = [ os . path . join ( "data" , str ( y )) for y in YEARS ] years_dirs_exist = all ( os . path . exists ( d ) for d in year_dirs ) if not os . path . exists ( "data" ) or not years_dirs_exist : return False else : filenames = generate_file_names () for f in filenames : if os . path . exists ( f ): os . remove ( f ) if years_dirs_exist : for d in year_dirs : os . rmdir ( d ) return True
docs.title: remove_files()

docs.text: Scrapes webpage content from web response context. Parameters: Name Type Description Default content website content from response object required Returns: Type Description dict dict[str, list[str]]: dictionary of column names and data scraped from the web-table Source code in fl_health_scraper/core.py def scrape_site ( web_response : requests . Response ) -> dict [ str , list [ str ]]: """Scrapes webpage content from web response context. Args: content: website content from response object Returns: dict[str, list[str]]: dictionary of column names and data scraped from the web-table """ content = web_response . content soup = BeautifulSoup ( content , "html.parser" ) table = soup . find ( id = "dtOpioidProfile" ) data : dict [ str , list [ str ]] = { "Indicator" : [], "Measure" : [], "Year" : [], "Jan-Mar" : [], "Apr-June" : [], "July-Sep" : [], "Oct-Dec" : [], "Year-to-Date" : [], "Case Definition" : [], } # get table data directly using regex compiled 'id' attribute regex_columns : list [ str ] = [ "colIndTitle_Row" , "colMeasure_Row" , "colYear_Row" , "colQuarter1_Row" , "colQuarter2_Row" , "colQuarter3_Row" , "colQuarter4_Row" , "colAnnual_Row" , "colCaseDefinition_Row" , ] # these loops can be improved (readability, refactored into funcs etc.) for field , col in zip ( data . keys (), regex_columns ): data . update ( { field : [ r . text . strip () for r in table . find_all ( id = re . compile ( f " { col } " ))]} ) return data
docs.title: scrape_site()

docs.text: Visit a specified site using post-request and return response content. Parameters: Name Type Description Default url str base url to visit required county_id int numeric id of county to query from form data required year int year to query in form data required Returns: Type Description Response bytes: response content Source code in fl_health_scraper/core.py def visit_site ( url : str , county_id : int , year : int ) -> requests . Response : """Visit a specified site using post-request and return response content. Args: url: base url to visit county_id: numeric id of county to query from form data year: year to query in form data Returns: bytes: response content """ response = requests . post ( url , data = { "islCounty" : county_id , "islYears" : year }) return response
